description: city in Indiana, United States
description_wiki: New Castle is a city in Henry County, Indiana, United States.

description: former US Army post in Indianapolis, IN, US 1906-1991
description_wiki: Fort Benjamin Harrison was a U.S. Army post located in suburban Lawrence Township, Marion County, Indiana, northeast of Indianapolis, between 1906 and 1991.

description: military base located in Virginia, United States of America
description_wiki: Fort Eustis is a United States Army installation in Newport News, Virginia.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Newport News () is an independent city in southeastern Virginia, United States.

description: census-designated place in the state of Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Fort Stewart is a United States Army post in the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: huge area of heavy fighting across Europe
U.S. Army camp in Taunton, Massachusettes
description_wiki: Camp Myles Standish was a U.S. Army camp located in Taunton, Massachusetts, during World War II. It was the main staging area for the Boston Port of Embarkation, with about a million U.S. and Allied soldiers passing through the camp on their way overseas or returning for demobilization after the war.

description: capital and largest city of Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Boston () is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Massachusetts.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Wales (Welsh: Cymru [ˈkəmrɨ] ) is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: village in Dorset, England, UK
description_wiki: Puddletown is a village in the civil parish of Athelhampton and Puddletown, in Dorset, England.

description: town in Dorset, England
description_wiki: Weymouth ( WAY-məth) is a seaside town and civil parish in the Dorset district, in the ceremonial county of Dorset, England.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: United States military award
description_wiki: The Good Conduct Medal is one of the oldest military awards of the United States Armed Forces.

description: military decoration of the United States
United States military decoration
description_wiki: The Purple Heart (PH) is a United States military decoration awarded in the name of the president to those wounded or killed while serving on or after 5 April 1917, with the U.S. military.

description: city in Marne, France
description_wiki: Reims ( REEMZ; French: [ʁɛ̃s] ; also spelled Rheims in English) is the most populous city in the French department of Marne, and the 12th most populous city in France.

description: city in Marne, France
description_wiki: Reims ( REEMZ; French: [ʁɛ̃s] ; also spelled Rheims in English) is the most populous city in the French department of Marne, and the 12th most populous city in France.

city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States
Veterans of Foreign Wars of the U.S.
description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW), formally the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States, is an organization of United States war veterans who fought in wars, campaigns, and expeditions on foreign land, waters, or airspace as military service members.

description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The American Legion, commonly known as the Legion, is an organization of U.S. veterans headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: city in and county seat of Cuyahoga County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Cleveland ( KLEEV-lənd) is a city in the U.S. state of Ohio and the county seat of Cuyahoga County.
